stem: In the figure (not drawn to scale), segments WZ and XY intersect at point Q, with ∠W and ∠Y each having measure a°. Given YQ = 12, WQ = 40, WX = 30, and XQ = 50, what is the length of YZ?
rationale: ∠WQX and ∠YQZ are vertical angles and therefore congruent. Combined with ∠W ≅ ∠Y, triangles QWX and QYZ are similar (AA), with correspondence W↔Y, X↔Z, Q↔Q. The similarity ratio is YQ/WQ = 12/40 = 3/10, so YZ = WX × 3/10 = 30 × 3/10 = 9.
